```{python}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| echo: false

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from matplotlib.ticker import FuncFormatter


def annuity_schedule(
    loan_amount: float,
    interest_rate: float,
    periods: int,
) -> tuple[float, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
    """Calculate the annuity, interest, and principal repayment."""
    annuity = (
        loan_amount
        * interest_rate
        / (1 - (1 + interest_rate) ** -periods)
    )

    interest = np.zeros(periods)
    redemption = np.zeros(periods)

    balance = loan_amount

    for period in range(periods):
        interest[period] = balance * interest_rate
        redemption[period] = min(annuity - interest[period], balance)
        balance -= redemption[period]

    return annuity, interest, redemption


loan_amount = 100_000
interest_rate = 0.07
periods = 20

annuity, interest, redemption = annuity_schedule(
    loan_amount,
    interest_rate,
    periods,
)

period = np.arange(1, periods + 1)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))

ax.bar(
    period,
    redemption,
    color="tab:blue",
    label="Principal repayment",
)
ax.bar(
    period,
    interest,
    bottom=redemption,
    color="tab:orange",
    label="Interest",
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Period",
    ylabel="Annual payment",
    title=f"Annuity loan (annual payment = €{annuity:,.2f})",
    xticks=period,
)

ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(
    FuncFormatter(lambda value, _: f"€{value:,.0f}")
)

ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.3)

fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
